page: Amazon Web Services c6gd.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and custom AWS Graviton processors. It features 48 physical ARM64 cores based on the Neoverse-N1 microarchitecture, running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz with a dedicated CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled as a 2850 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance rated in the top 25% at 72,845.21. Static web serving throughput is also strong, reaching 39.7 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is relatively modest, with Geekbench single-core scores and single-threaded gzip compression falling into the bottom 25%. Multi-threaded decompression and database operations perform in the average tier, as does LLM inference for small and medium models.

The c6gd.12xlarge offers a cost-efficient profile for multi-threaded workloads that benefit from high physical core density and local NVMe storage without the overhead of hyperthreading. The lack of AVX instruction sets makes it less suitable for specialized vector math, but it is highly effective for web serving, multi-threaded data processing, and database workloads requiring fast local scratch space.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated ARM64 cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processors running at 2.5 GHz. It includes 96.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 2850 GB NVMe SSD local instance store, supported by a 20 Gbps baseline network interface. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers strong multi-core performance, as demonstrated by high multi-core stress-ng scores and static web serving throughput of 39.7 GB/sec. Single-core performance is more modest, with lower single-threaded compression and Geekbench scores. Given its high physical core count and fast local storage, the c6gd.12xlarge is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and database workloads that require high local I/O performance without relying on x86-specific vector instructions.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 48 physical cores, 96 GB RAM, and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and web serving performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 48 physical cores, 96 GB RAM, and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized platform featuring forty-eight dedicated physical cores and high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
96.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Bundled 2850 GB local NVMe SSD instance storage
Strong multi-core CPU and static web serving benchmark performance
Best suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and databases
